page.content: In Table 1, the observations corresponding to the score are as follows: for Score 0, the observation is "No discernible clinical signs"; for Score 1, the observation is "Hunched back, slightly ruffled fur"; for Score 2, the observation is "Very ruffled fur, reduced rate of movement, developing motor impairments"; for Score 3, the observation is "Very ruffled fur, impaired balance/coordination, hypothermic, severe motor impairments such as ataxia, hemiplegia and paraplegia, convulsions, fitting"; for Score 4, the observation is "Very little movement, convulsions, fitting"; and for Score 5, the observation is "Loss of consciousness/coma." In Table 2, the perfusion distances (mm) with corresponding measurements are as follows: at 0 (n = 4), the volume size is 53.0 ± 15.0 cm3, axial diameter is 6.2 ± 1.0 cm, minimum transverse diameter is 4.0 ± 0.4 cm, maximum transverse diameter is 4.0 ± 0.4 cm, and sphericity is 1.5 ± 0.2; at 2 (n = 5), the volume size is 91.7 ± 18.2 cm3, axial diameter is 6.0 ± 0.2 cm, minimum transverse diameter is 5.3 ± 0.5 cm, maximum transverse diameter is 5.5 ± 0.5 cm, and sphericity is 1.1 ± 0.1; at 4 (n = 6), the volume size is 63.9 ± 11.7 cm3, axial diameter is 5.2 ± 0.5 cm, minimum transverse diameter is 4.5 ± 0.8 cm, maximum transverse diameter is 5.2 ± 0.5 cm, and sphericity is 1.1 ± 0.2, with P* values of <0.05 for volume size, minimum and maximum transverse diameters, and <0.01 for sphericity, while axial diameter is non-significant (NS).
